title: Gomesa alvesiana
intro: Gomesa alvesiana, also known as a type of orchid, is a species of flowering plant native to Brazil, characterized by its unique and intricate blooms. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health issues, including inflammation, fever, and gastrointestinal problems, due to its potential health benefits. The bioactive constituents of Gomesa alvesiana include flavonoids, alkaloids, and glycosides, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with this plant include infusions and tinctures, which are used to treat a range of health conditions, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy.
taxonomy: Gomesa alvesiana is classified as a species of orchid in the family Orchidaceae. It belongs to the subfamily Epidendroideae and the tribe Cymbidieae.
